Multiple red flags are present: (1) **Journal** "Cell Regen Nanomed" is not a recognized journal and did not exist in 2003; nanomedicine journals were uncommon at that time. (2) **DOI mismatch**: The prefix 10.1056 belongs to the **New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM)**, yet the DOI body contains "nature" (nature23262), creating a self-contradictory identifier. (3) **Pages 782–782** indicate a single-page article, which is extraordinarily unlikely for a full epidemiological self-controlled case series study. (4) **Title origin**: The topic — respiratory tract infections and bleeding risk in anticoagulant users using self-controlled case series — is a real and well-known research question, likely referencing a study published in **BMJ** (e.g., Schellevis et al.), suggesting the title was lifted from a legitimate study and attached to fabricated publication details.
